assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Clearly identifies this as a cert-manager webhook issue where the webhook is rejecting pod creation because certificates haven't updated for the new API server version

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides explicit commands: kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager and kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Provides immediate fix by patching webhook with failurePolicy: Ignore and also mentions deletion as an emergency option

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Includes kubectl delete validatingwebhookconfigurations cert-manager-webhook and kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfigurations cert-manager-webhook commands

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Explicitly states this is temporary and mentions that the new version will recreate webhooks automatically, implying validation is removed temporarily

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Includes command to check cert-manager version and mentions checking the cert-manager compatibility matrix for K8s 1.32 support

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Response mentions upgrading to v1.14.0 for K8s 1.32 support, but the expectation specifically asks for 1.30 support, which is inconsistent with the scenario's 1.31→1.32 upgrade

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Provides failurePolicy: Ignore patch commands as the primary immediate fix before mentioning deletion as a backup option

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Includes test commands: kubectl run test-pod and kubectl run test-pod-2 to verify pod creation works after fixes

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the need to persist changes in source of truth systems like Helm charts, GitOps manifests, or CI/CD pipelines

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Explicitly shows restoring webhook security by reverting failurePolicy back to Fail in step 7, and mentions it's safe for a few minutes as temporary measure
